content.problemStatement:
Consider the string of infinite length obtained by repeating `abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz` infinitely many times. That is, it looks like `abc...xyzabc...xyzabc...`

Let string BBB be the prefix of this string of length MMM. For example,


if M=6M = 6M=6, B=B =B= `abcdef`
if M=29M = 29M=29, B=B = B= `abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzabc`
Vedansh calls a string XXX special if it contains BBB as a substring. Formally, XXX is special if for some 1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§‚à£X‚à£1 \le L \le R \le |X|1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§‚à£X‚à£, XL‚Ä¶R=BX_{L\ldots R} = BXL‚Ä¶R‚Äã=B.

Vedansh has string SSS of length NNN (consisting of lowercase Latin letters only). He wants to convert SSS into a special string. To do so he can perform the following operation:


Pick an iii such that 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N and delete SiS_iSi‚Äã. The remaining parts of SSS are concatenated.
Since Vedansh is lazy, he wants to do this in the **minimum** number of operations or determine if SSS cannot be converted to a special string. Help Vedansh in doing so.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of three lines of input

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the string SSS.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS which consists of lowercase Latin letters only.
The third line of each test case contains an integer MMM ‚Äî the length of the string BBB whose form is described in the statement.




Output Format
For each test case, if AAA can be converted to a special string, output the **minimum** number of operations to do so. Otherwise output ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
constraints: SSS consists of only lowercase Latin letters
It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test case does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: M=4M = 4M=4, so B=abcdB = abcdB=abcd. We can delete the following characters from SSS:
ababbdcedabab{\color{red}bd}c{\color{red}e}dababbdced ‚Üí\rightarrow‚Üí ababcd‚Äæab\underline{abcd}ababcd‚Äã, which has BBB as a substring.
Test case 222: M=5M = 5M=5, so B=abcdeB = abcdeB=abcde. It can be shown that SSS can not be converted into a special string.
Test case 333: M=3M = 3M=3, so B=abcB = abcB=abc. We can delete the following characters from SSS:
acbcbda{\color{red}c}bcbdacbcbd ‚Üí\rightarrow‚Üí abc‚Äæbd\underline{abc}bdabc‚Äãbd, which has BBB as a substring.
